subject: Statistics
title: Credit Scoring
description: A micro-lesson that makes Credit Scoring usable.

hook.text: A three-digit number determines if you get a mortgage, a credit card, or a loan. How is it calculated?

buildup.text: Credit scores use logistic regression on payment history, debt levels, account age, and credit mix.

discovery.text: The model predicts the probability of default in the next two years. Higher score means lower risk.

twist.text: The exact formula is proprietary. Billions of dollars in lending decisions rest on a model nobody can audit.

climax.text: Regression models shape real lives. A 20-point score change can mean thousands in interest rates.

punchline.text: A regression model decides who gets a loan. For better or worse.

quiz.question: What does a credit score predict?
options: The probability of defaulting on a loan within a specific time period
How much money you currently have in savings
